channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Roberta Flack's 'Celebration of Life' memorial to feature Stevie Wonder and Rev. Al Sharpton
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Stevie Wonder, the Reverend Al Sharpton and the Nebulous String Quartet from Berklee College of Music will help celebrate the life and legacy of Roberta Flack at a memorial Monday in New York City.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Credit card debt calculator: Find out how much you owe
item.description: Americans have more credit card debt than ever. Figure out how much you owe and how long it will take to pay back.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: 3 victims of Pennsylvania plane crash were taken to a burn unit for treatment, hospital says
item.description: LITITZ, Pa. (AP) — Three of the five people injured in a single-engine plane crash in central Pennsylvania over the weekend were sent from a Lancaster hospital to a burn center for treatment, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump DOT chief rejects inclusion of environmental justice factors in infrastructure decisions
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy on Monday rescinded Biden-era memos that he said called on state agencies to factor a “social justice and environmental agenda” into infrastructure project decisions.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Scholars stranded in America and abroad amid funding freeze of State Department programs
item.description: Fulbright grant recipient Aubrey Lay was supposed to get paid for three months of work by the U.S. government through his teaching assistantship at a school for Ukrainian refugees in Estonia. Instead, he only got about one week's pay and no word on when he might see the rest of his grant.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stocks plunge as Wall Street wonders how much economic pain Trump will accept
item.description: The economy has already given some signals of weakening, mostly through surveys showing increased pessimism.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Convicted Pentagon leaker Jack Teixeira faces court-martial while already serving 15-year sentence
item.description: Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, convicted of leaking Pentagon documents on the Ukraine war, faces court-martial Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspects accused of Iran-backed plot to kill journalist on American soil face trial
item.description: Two suspects are facing trial in New York on Monday after being charged in a thwarted plot to kill Iranian-American journalist Masih Alinejad on U.S. soil.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court takes up Colorado conversion therapy case
item.description: The Supreme Court granted a Colorado therapist’s request to review her challenge to a state law banning mental health care providers from engaging minors in conversion therapy, a discredited practice that attempts to change a person’s sexual orientation or gender identity to align with heterosexual
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: Why these 2 states didn't 'spring forward' to daylight saving time this morning
item.description: You may have been groggy this morning thanks to our clocks “springing forward” an hour overnight. Unless, of course, you live in either of the two states that don’t observe daylight saving time.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Oldest former MLB player, who once replaced Mickey Mantle on Yankees' roster, dies
item.description: Art Schallock got his shot in the major leagues after a struggling Mickey Mantle was optioned by New York in 1951.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas bill could charge transgender people with 'gender identity fraud', if passed
item.description: A bill filed in the Texas House of Representatives this week threatens to charge transgender Texans with a crime for identifying with a gender that differs from their sex assigned at birth.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy arrested for assault in Texas
item.description: On Friday, Chiefs star rookie Xavier Worthy, 21, was arrested in Williamson County, Texas after investigators said he assaulted a family member or person in his household by "impeding their breathing or circulation.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Pickleball isn't named after a dog: How the sport really earned its moniker
item.description: You may have heard a rumor about the game being named after a dog. Spoiler alert: That isn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: A total lunar eclipse and blood moon will be visible in March: How and when to watch
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow during a total lunar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Watch the moon turn red during a total lunar eclipse in March
item.description: A total lunar eclipse will flush the moon red Thursday night into Friday morning across the Western Hemisphere.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN AP Science Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Storm dumps heavy snow on northern Arizona, gridlocking Interstate 40
item.description: A winter storm dumped heavy snow across northern Arizona on Friday, playing a major factor in a more than 15-mile (24-kilometer) backup on a major interstate.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Venezuelan parents watch from home as their son is laid to rest in Texas after drowning
item.description: EAGLE PASS, Texas (AP) — The cries of Liliana Olivero watching from Venezuela through a cellphone pierced through the somber Christian music and gusts of wind as her son's casket was lowered into the ground.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, ABC 4

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: Raiders acquire quarterback Geno Smith from Seahawks for a 3rd-round pick, AP source says
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders answered their most pressing personnel question of the offseason Friday by acquiring quarterback Geno Smith in a trade with Seattle and reuniting him with his former coach, a person with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CDC launches 'conflicts of interest' page about vaccine advisory panel
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) published a page on Friday listing the conflicts of interest reported by members of a key vaccine advisory committee.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reopening Texas detention centers to hold deportees worries migrant advocates
item.description: Two controversial immigration detention facilities in South Texas are being reopened for the detention and deportation of migrant women and families, officials tell Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge won't order immigration officials to change school arrests policy
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Immigration authorities don't need to revert to a Biden-era policy limiting arrests at schools after officials in Denver challenged new policies from the Trump administration, a federal judge ruled Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chick-fil-A introduces new spring menu items, brings back fan-favorite sandwich
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the arrival of spring with new seasonal menu items, including a returning fan favorite.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Lawsuit aims to strike down LGBTQ antidiscrimination protections in Pennsylvania
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Two public school districts and several parents have sued Pennsylvania in a bid to undo antidiscrimination protections for gay and transgender people, saying the state's two-year-old regulation is illegal because it goes beyond what lawmakers intended or allowed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mexican cartel leader’s son is sentenced to life in prison for role in major drug trafficking plot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Mexican drug cartel boss was sentenced Friday to life in prison for helping his father run one of the country’s largest and most violent narcotics trafficking organizations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: States sue President Trump’s administration over mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Maryland and 19 other states are suing multiple federal agencies, contending President Donald Trump's administration has illegally fired thousands of federal probationary workers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order creating bitcoin reserve
item.description: Trump, who once called crypto a "scam," has drastically changed his tune on the subject and promised on the campaign trail to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, Fox 5

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4
